content.problemStatement:
On the occasion of Yoga Day, the only yoga instructor in Chefland, Chef, has received numerous queries.

Chef has managed to free up NNN hours from his busy schedule to conduct yoga sessions. There are two types of sessions that Chef offers:


Type 111 session, which lasts 111 hour, and earns Chef XXX rupees.
Type 222 session, which lasts 222 hours, and earns Chef Y (Y>X)Y \ (Y \gt X)Y (Y>X) rupees.
Find the **maximum** amount of money Chef can earn in NNN hours.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three space-separated integers N,X,YN, X, YN,X,Y — the number of hours for which Chef will take yoga sessions, price of type 111 session, and price of type 222 session respectively.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** amount of money Chef can earn in NNN hours.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Chef will take type 111 sessions for all 444 hours. Thus, in 444 hours, he can take 444 sessions earning a total of 4⋅5=204\cdot 5 = 204⋅5=20 rupees.
Test case 222: Chef will take one session of type 111 and one session of type 222. Thus, in 333 hours, he earns a total of 1⋅1+1⋅2=31\cdot 1 + 1\cdot 2 = 31⋅1+1⋅2=3 rupees.
Test case 333: Chef will take one session of type 111 and two sessions of type 222. Thus, in 555 hours, he earns a total of 1⋅2+2⋅6=141\cdot 2 + 2\cdot 6 = 141⋅2+2⋅6=14 rupees.
